bead.title: runs backend: GraphQL Run type with RunLayer enum + runs/run queries + resolvers
bead.description:
Add the layer-aware Run type and runs query to schema.graphql, backed by the FEAT-010 file store (.ddx/exec/).

NOTE: Naming distinction — existing ExecutionRun type in schema maps to exec-definition runs (test/check results). The new Run type is the three-layer work/try/run substrate from the agent execute workflow. They are separate types.

Run type fields:
- id, layer (enum: work, try, run), status, startedAt, completedAt
- beadId, artifactId, parentRunId, childRunIds
- Work-layer fields: queueInputs, stopCondition, selectedBeadIds
- Try-layer fields (inline fragment / nullable): beadId, baseRevision, resultRevision, worktreePath, mergeOutcome, checkResults
- Run-layer fields (inline fragment / nullable): promptSummary, powerMin, powerMax, harness, provider, model, tokensIn, tokensOut, costUsd, durationMs, outputExcerpt, evidenceLinks

GraphQL:
- runs(projectID, layer, beadID, status, harness, first, after): RunConnection
- run(id): Run

Resolver reads from .ddx/exec/runs/ (FEAT-010 exec store, file-backed). For work/try layer records read from .ddx/executions/ attempt records.
bead.acceptance:
1. Run type and RunLayer enum present in schema.graphql.
2. runs query returns paginated results; layer filter works (work/try/run).
3. run(id) returns single record with correct layer-specific fields populated.
4. Parent/child relationships correctly resolved from file store.
5. ExecutionRun type unchanged — no naming collision.
6. Go resolver tests cover layer filtering, parent/child links, and missing run id (returns nil not error).
